title: Arithmetic theta series
speaker: Stephan Kudla
abstract: Abstract: In recent joint work with Jan Bruinier, Ben Howard, Michael Rapoport and Tonghai Yang, we proved that a certain generating series for the classes of arithmetic divisors on a regular integral model M of a Shimura variety for a unitary group of signature (n-1,1) for an imaginary quadratic field is a modular form of weight n valued in the first arithmetic Chow group of M. I will discuss how this is proved, highlighting the main steps.

proof_stages.label: Theta-correspondence motivation for arithmetic theta series
proof_stages.summary: The talk reviews classical theta kernels and Kudla--Millson geometric theta forms, then formulates the programme of replacing cohomology-valued theta series by arithmetic-Chow-valued theta series whose nonvanishing should reflect derivatives of L-functions.

proof_stages.label: Unitary Shimura variety and regular integral model
proof_stages.summary: The speaker fixes an imaginary quadratic field, Hermitian spaces W and W0, level lattices, and the unitary group, identifies the resulting Shimura variety with a moduli problem for abelian varieties, and constructs the Pappas and Krämer integral models with toroidal compactifications.

proof_stages.label: Special divisors from norm-m homomorphism loci
proof_stages.summary: For a moduli point (A0,A), the OK-linear homomorphisms HomOK(A0,A) carry a natural positive Hermitian form, and the locus of triples with norm m defines a finite unramified Cartier divisor Z(m).

proof_stages.label: Cusps, boundary lattices, and corrected total divisors
proof_stages.summary: The toroidal boundary is described via rational isotropic lines and their associated positive definite cusp lattices, and the Zariski closures of the special divisors are corrected by explicit boundary components to form total divisors Ztot(m).

proof_stages.label: Arithmetic Chow classes, constant term, and Green functions
proof_stages.summary: The total divisors are promoted to arithmetic Chow classes by specifying Green functions, while the m=0 term is defined through the metrized tautological line bundle together with exceptional and vertical corrections.

proof_stages.label: Modularity theorem and arithmetic theta lift
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem asserts that the formal generating series of these arithmetic Chow classes is a holomorphic modular form of weight n, level D, and character chiK^n, yielding finite-dimensionality consequences and arithmetic theta lifts of cusp forms.

proof_stages.label: Borcherds--Serre-duality reduction to arithmetic relations
proof_stages.summary: Using Borcherds' modularity criterion, the proof reduces modularity to arithmetic Chow relations supplied by Borcherds products pulled back from an orthogonal Shimura variety, with Bruinier-type regularized theta lifts aligning the Green functions and leaving only explicit modular correction series.

proof_stages.label: Fourier--Jacobi boundary computation of Borcherds divisors
proof_stages.summary: The hard divisor formula is obtained by analyzing the pulled-back Borcherds product near the toroidal boundary through Fourier--Jacobi expansions along curve boundary components, reducing the integral boundary divisor calculation to divisors of Jacobi theta functions and explaining the use of both Pappas and Krämer models.

examples.name: Ball quotients and Hodge-conjecture applications
examples.note: The case of Hermitian symmetric spaces such as U(n,1) ball quotients illustrates how geometric theta forms and special cycles can produce algebraic-cycle results, motivating the arithmetic analogue without being used in its proof.
examples.engagement_reason: He cites ball quotients as cases where prior theta-cycle machinery was used to prove Hodge-conjecture results, but does not compute or run a construction on that example.

passes.reason: Ball quotients are locally Hermitian symmetric U(n,1) quotients used as an application to Hodge-conjecture cases, not as a failure case.

examples.name: Upper-half-plane rational cusps analogy
examples.note: The familiar modular-curve picture of adjoining only rational cusps is used to make the rational isotropic boundary components of the unitary Shimura variety concrete.
examples.engagement_reason: He uses rational cusps of the upper half plane only as an analogy for rational boundary components, without analyzing the upper-half-plane case itself.

passes.reason: The rational cusps are boundary points of the symmetric upper half-plane, invoked only as an analogy for rational boundary components.

examples.name: CM-cycle pullback from a totally real field
examples.note: A totally real field and its composite CM field give an arithmetic curve whose pullback degrees of the theta-lift classes lead to pairings with derivatives of incoherent Eisenstein series, illustrating the kind of arithmetic information the theorem can access.
examples.engagement_reason: He spells out the CM-cycle construction from a totally real field, embeds the CM moduli stack as an arithmetic curve, pulls back theta classes, takes arithmetic degrees, and identifies the resulting pairing with a derivative Eisenstein-series integral.

passes.reason: The CM pullback cycle is a standard special-cycle construction in an infinite family, used to compute degrees rather than to refute a claim.
The CM-cycle is a special cycle of abelian varieties with maximal CM endomorphisms, used to pull back arithmetic theta classes and compute degrees, not to disprove anything.
